headline_drivers: Street re-rating of HBM/AI memory earnings power after Micron’s late‑March FY26 guidance and commentary on multi‑year AI demand, driving a sustained AI‑infrastructure rerating across memory names (late March–April 2026)

BofA Securities raises Micron price target to $950 from $500 and reiterates Buy, explicitly tying upside to AI server/HBM demand and expanding margin profile (reported in market coverage around 2026-05-13)

Ongoing sector momentum from AI compute and data‑center peers (e.g., NVIDIA, Broadcom, other AI infrastructure beneficiaries) with investors rotating into memory as a leveraged way to play hyperscaler AI capex (March–May 2026)
summary: The recent 60–80% move in Micron isn’t about a one‑day headline but a rapid re-rating of its AI/HBM earnings power, reinforced by bullish sell-side calls like BofA’s target hike to $950 that frame MU as a core beneficiary of hyperscaler AI build‑outs. Investors are extrapolating Micron’s earlier guidance and commentary on tight high‑bandwidth memory supply, AI server content growth, and improving margins into a much higher multi‑year EPS trajectory, which is why the stock has squeezed sharply higher on elevated volume and a very high RSI. The -5–6% pullback on the day looks more like digestion/positioning after a parabolic run (profit-taking, short-covering reversing) than a response to new negative fundamentals. In short, the market is reacting to an AI-infrastructure-driven earnings upgrade cycle and aggressive upward revisions to fair value, not to a fresh company-specific press release in the last 24 hours.
